publisherFrame.boundary: This is a published technical reference model. It defines terminology, validation behavior, and runtime interpretation. It does not claim legal, governmental, constitutional, or regulatory authority.

modelPurpose: Define how DrMarchand’s ∞ OS™ distinguishes fact, fiction, realistic possibility, drift, and false authority before writing v0.0.7 path containment verification.
nextBigMove: Write v0.0.7 as path containment verification, not merely line-or-curve verification.
corePrinciple: Fact has anchor. Fiction has label. Drift has boundary. Uncontained drift has refusal.

stateInterpretation.fact: Anchored, traceable, bounded, testable, and classified.
stateInterpretation.realisticPossibility: Not yet proven as fact, but carrying enough anchor behavior to remain under review.
stateInterpretation.fiction: Unreal or creative material that remains safe when labeled and contained.
stateInterpretation.drift: Movement away from anchor, lineage, boundary, or classification.
stateInterpretation.uncontainedDrift: Drift that is allowed to influence structure without boundary, classification, or pairing.
stateInterpretation.falseAuthority: Uncontained drift pretending to be fact or source authority.

boundedNumberModel.realNumberMeaning: Real number means a value in ℝ, the mathematical real-number line. It does not mean merely a whole number or a number without decimals.
boundedNumberModel.rule: A bounded maximum is not infinity. It is the highest allowed value inside a chosen model.

validationRules: A possible cycle is not a confirmed cycle until its path demonstrates bounded return.
Six begins the path; seven reads the path.
A pair does not need even inputs; a pair needs declared relationship and valid resolution.
Only what returns genuinely may be carried forward toward 0.1.0.
